alerts.message: Disk at 91% capacity — 20Gi remaining of 228Gi. At current accumulation rate (approx 3Gi/week from logs + cron artifacts), system has ~6 weeks before write failures begin. Recommend immediate cleanup or archival.
n8n experienced a DOWN event 2026-06-11 11:01-16:02 UTC (5h outage). Currently recovered and healthy (heartbeat 200). Herald sync may have been blocked during window.

[SKILL] macOS Storage Cleanup (monthly) — status 'null', never executed since creation. 2 cron jobs with status 'null': storage cleanup (job 3a4e5fd94686) and new Companion Briefing variant (job c74958c1070e).
No inbound Telegram messages since 2026-06-09 (3 days). Gateway last confirmed connected 2026-06-09T21:44 UTC. May be expected idle period, but worth monitoring.

outsider.quote: "The hermit who watches from the ridge does not knock — he counts the days until the door opens from the other side."
observations: Gateway healthy, telegram connected. Disk at 90% — 21Gi left of 228Gi. Memory idle at 78%. n8n heartbeat alive (200, 2 attempts). No alarms, but the margins are thinning.
outsider.insight: The system breathes quietly tonight — no alarms, no errors, no panicked retries. But the disk is a slow wound: 90% consumed, 21Gi remaining. At the current rate of memory-log growth and cron output accumulation, the system has perhaps 14–18 days before write failures begin. The gateway remains the single point of fragility — one process, one PID, one connection threading the entire conversation. Tonight it holds, but the architecture asks: what happens when it doesn't?
